sections.about: This metric reports employee fatalities from work-related injury during the reporting year. It falls under BRSR Principle 3, Essential Indicator 11. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Tata reported 1 employee fatality in FY23, 0 in FY24 and 1 in FY25. JSW and Jindal reported 0 in all three years, while SAIL reported 1 in each year. Employee fatalities are low-frequency but high-severity events, so annual ranking is sensitive to single incidents. Tata’s FY25 disclosure identifies fatality risk control, process safety management, PSSR strengthening and real-time equipment monitoring as part of the safety agenda, but the recurrence from 0 to 1 shows that fatality-risk controls still need deeper horizontal deployment.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranked 3rd at 1 fatality, behind JSW and Jindal at 0. Percentage gap is not meaningful because the best value is zero; the absolute gap is 1 fatality. Tata’s rank improved to joint 1st in FY24 but reverted in FY25.
sections.targets: Tata states zero harm as the safety ambition. Jindal states Zero Harm.
